summary: Implemented entry point in cmd/task-dashboard/main.go with full startup sequence: CLI flags (--config, --port), config loading, scanner creation, Gin engine setup with page/API routes and validation middleware, embedded static assets via go:embed with fs.Sub for /static/ prefix, templates from embedded web/templates, server binding to 127.0.0.1 only, and graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM. Extracted testable setupRouter and defaultConfigPath functions. Binary builds as single executable and serves HTML landing page, static JS/CSS, and JSON API.
keyDecisions: Extracted setupRouter() from main() for testability - router setup is fully unit-testable without signal handling
Used fs.Sub(web.Assets, 'static') to serve only static/ subdirectory, preventing template leakage via /static/
Server binds to 127.0.0.1 only (not 0.0.0.0) for security - localhost-only access
Gin ReleaseMode set by default for production; uses gin.New() with Recovery() middleware
net.Listen explicitly in goroutine before srv.Serve to ensure port is available before signal wait
Graceful shutdown with 5-second timeout using context.WithTimeout
CLI --port flag overrides config file port; --config flag overrides default ~/.task-dashboard.yaml path

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Config loaded from ~/.task-dashboard.yaml or --config CLI flag
Port configurable via config or --port CLI flag (default 8080)
Gin engine created with page handlers, API handlers, validation middleware
Static assets served from embedded web/static/ via go:embed
Templates loaded from embedded web/templates/ via go:embed
go build -o task-dashboard ./cmd/task-dashboard/ produces single binary
Binary starts and responds to GET / with HTML
Binary serves embedded static JS/CSS files

notes: Coverage for cmd/task-dashboard is 27.9% because main() and runServer() involve os/signal blocking and log.Fatalf which cannot be unit-tested in-process. The testable functions (setupRouter, defaultConfigPath) are fully covered. Integration test confirmed binary starts, responds to GET / with HTML, serves static JS/CSS, and handles /api/projects. All 13 unit tests verify router setup, static file serving, template rendering, graceful shutdown, localhost binding, and embedded asset availability.
